Receipt OCR Pipeline - Validation Gallery

Ground truth validation: 17 receipts hand-verified with image-vs-extraction side-by-side. Every field spot-checked - prices, quantities, units, tax flags. Confidence scores are self-reported by the model; this gallery is the external proof they're trustworthy.

Receipts Processed

Validation Results

Hand-Verified Accuracy

Confidence scores are self-reported by the model. These numbers are from manual spot-checks against actual receipt images.

17/17
Supplier Names Correct
17/17
Receipt Totals Match
100%
Tax/Discount Flags Correct
2
Items Flagged for Review

The 2 flagged items are partial reads on faded thermal paper - correctly marked as medium confidence rather than guessing.

Understanding the Numbers

What Each Metric Means

Every number in this report is computed from raw receipt data with full traceability. Here's exactly how.

OCR Confidence
96.7%
How certain Claude Vision is about each extracted field. Each line item gets a confidence level (high/medium/low) based on text clarity, field completeness, and extraction certainty. The 96.7% is the weighted average across all 318 line items.
confidence = weighted_avg(per_field_confidence)
high = 0.95, medium = 0.80, low = 0.60
Why it matters: Low confidence items get flagged for manual review instead of silently polluting your cost data.
Mapping Rate
94.9%
The percentage of extracted line items that successfully mapped to a known ingredient. Uses 3-tier matching: manual overrides (exact), fuzzy string matching (thefuzz, threshold 80), then LLM batch mapping for remaining items.
mapping_rate = mapped_items / total_items
3-tier: overrides > fuzzy(80) > LLM batch
Why it matters: Unmapped items (5.1%) include discounts, delivery fees, and cleaning supplies - correctly excluded from COGS.
Margin %
73.7%
The profit margin after subtracting total COGS (ingredient + packaging) from sell price. Averaged across all 12 menu items. A cafe target is typically 65-75%, so 73.7% is excellent.
margin = (sell_price - total_cogs) / sell_price * 100
total_cogs = ingredient_cost + packaging_cost
Why it matters: Items below 60% margin (like Butter Croissant at 59.6%) are candidates for price increases or recipe optimization.
Item Confidence
0.86-0.95
Per-menu-item confidence combines three signals: OCR confidence of source data, mapping confidence (how well receipt items matched ingredients), and data volume (more receipts = more confidence). Penalized when coefficient of variation exceeds 30%.
conf = min(0.95, ocr_conf * map_conf * vol_factor)
if CV > 0.30: conf *= 0.85 # penalty
Why it matters: Berry Smoothie has the lowest confidence (0.86) because its ingredient costs vary widely across suppliers - the 95% CI spans 39.5%-83.0% margin.
COGS (Cost of Goods Sold)
EUR 0.41-2.13
The total cost to make one menu item, including raw ingredients and packaging. Computed as weighted average of all receipt data for each ingredient, after removing outliers using IQR method (k=1.5).
unit_cost = weighted_avg(prices, after IQR removal)
cogs = sum(ingredient_qty * unit_cost) + packaging
Why it matters: Even small COGS changes compound. A EUR 0.10 increase on Cappuccino across 50 daily sales = EUR 150/month lost margin.
Sensitivity (95% CI)
±1.96 SD
How much margins could swing if ingredient costs shift. Uses standard deviation of observed prices to compute 95% confidence intervals. The wider the CI, the more volatile that item's profitability.
95% CI = cogs ± 1.96 * std_dev
worst_case_margin = (price - (cogs + 1.96*SD)) / price
Why it matters: Berry Smoothie's worst case drops to 50.2% margin - still profitable but worth monitoring. Espresso barely moves because coffee bean prices are consistent.
Architecture

8-Stage Async Pipeline

Each receipt flows through these stages with typed Pydantic contracts between them

1 OCR
2 Validate
3 Normalize
4 Classify
5 Map
6 Calculate
7 Persist
8 Report
Real-World Challenges

What Receipts Actually Look Like

Real supplier invoices aren't clean PDFs. They're faded, stained, crumpled, and photographed at weird angles. Here's how the pipeline handles each scenario.

Faded Thermal Print
Old thermal receipt where ink has faded significantly - common after 2-3 months in a drawer
How we handle it: Claude Vision compensates with context clues. Tesseract fails below 0.60 confidence on these.
Tilted Phone Capture
Receipt photographed at an angle on a cluttered desk - the most common real-world scenario
How we handle it: Vision model handles up to ~15 deg rotation natively. Tesseract needs deskew preprocessing.
Worn Thermal Paper
Thermal paper with horizontal streaks and salt-and-pepper noise from a dying printer
How we handle it: Pipeline assigns "medium" confidence. Costs get wider standard deviations in sensitivity analysis.
Coffee-Stained Receipt
Receipt with a coffee ring obscuring part of the text - happens in every cafe
How we handle it: Partial occlusion. Vision model reads around the stain; missing fields marked low confidence.
Crumpled & Smoothed
Receipt that was balled up and then flattened - wrinkle shadows create false edges
How we handle it: Wrinkle artifacts cause Tesseract to hallucinate characters. Vision model handles this well.
Torn Bottom Edge
Receipt torn at the bottom - total and payment method missing
How we handle it: Pipeline reconstructs total from line items. Flags as needs-review due to missing validation.
Flash Overexposure
Phone flash reflecting off glossy paper - center is blown out white
How we handle it: Vision model reads edges. Center items may have lower confidence or be missing entirely.
Mixed Language Invoice
French supplier with localized product names and EU formatting - common in Belgium
How we handle it: LLM mapping handles cross-language: "Muffins Myrtille" maps to blueberry_muffin regardless.
Data Edge Cases

Edge Cases in the Data

Beyond image quality, the data itself has surprises the pipeline must handle correctly.

DE / FR / ES
Multi-Language OCR
German, French, Spanish invoices with localized product names
"Espresso Mischung" and "Leche Entera" both map correctly via LLM batch mapping
-EUR 47.50
Credit Notes & Refunds
Negative totals from returned goods
Detected by sign, marked as credit note, excluded from cost averaging
-5% / -10%
Discounts & Fees
Percentage discounts, delivery charges, bulk pricing
is_discount and is_tax_or_fee flags exclude from ingredient costs
$ / GBP / EUR
Multi-Currency
USD and GBP invoices alongside EUR
Normalized to EUR at configurable fixed rates (USD=0.92, GBP=1.17)
Soap / Napkins
Non-COGS Filtering
Cleaning supplies and operational items mixed in with food ingredients
Classification stage separates COGS from supplies; non-COGS items stay unmapped
box/24, 6pk, 10L
Unit Normalization
Suppliers use different units: boxes, packs, liters, kilograms
Normalize to per-unit cost: box/24 at EUR 23.64 becomes EUR 0.985/croissant
